---
title: "02.pruebas"
author: "Leyre Franquet"
date: "2026-02-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

```{r}
### CE ###
library(data.table)
setwd("/home/lpstroke/Documentos/Leyre Franquet/proyecto_tfg/gen1-2/Both")
## cargo las dos dataframes, la de stroke (la general) y la de CE (una subetiología)
ce <- fread("Both/CE_snptest_both_clean.txt",
            select = c("rsid","chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB",
                       "frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"))

stroke <- fread("Both/STROKE_snptest_both_clean.txt",
                select = c("rsid","chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB",
                           "frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"))
# arreglo los nombres de las columnas para que quede más claro
setnames(ce,
         old = c("frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"),
         new = c("beta_CE","se_CE","p_CE"))

setnames(stroke,
         old = c("frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"),
         new = c("beta_STROKE","se_STROKE","p_STROKE"))

# uno por SNP
merged <- merge(
  ce, stroke,
  by = c("chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB"),
  all = FALSE
)

head(merged)
dim(merged)
# PARA SABER EL % DE LA MISMA DIRECCIÓN DEL EFECTO
merged[, same_direction := sign(beta_CE) == sign(beta_STROKE)]
mean(merged$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
# CORRELACIÓN DE BETAS PARA VER CÓMO DE PARECIDAS SON
cor(merged$beta_CE, merged$beta_STR, use = "complete.obs")
# VER SI LOS SNP MÁS SIGNIFICATIVOS DE CE, REPLICAN EN STROKE
top_ce <- merged[order(p_CE)][1:1000]
mean(top_ce$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
summary(top_ce$p_STROKE)


hits_ce <- merged[p_CE < 1e-6][order(p_CE)]
hits_ce[, .(chromosome, position, alleleA, alleleB, beta_CE, p_CE, beta_STROKE, p_STROKE)][1:30]

#crear PLOT
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(1)
plot_df <- merged[sample(.N, 200000)]  # muestreo para que no reviente el plot

ggplot(plot_df, aes(beta_STROKE, beta_CE)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  labs(x = "Beta STROKE (global)", y = "Beta CE",
       title = "Comparación de efectos: CE vs STROKE")

```
```{r}
### LAA ###
library(data.table)
setwd("/home/lpstroke/Documentos/Leyre Franquet/proyecto_tfg/gen1-2/Both")
getwd()
## cargo las dos dataframes, la de stroke (la general) y la de CE (una subetiología)
laa <- fread("Both/LAA_snptest_both_clean.txt",
            select = c("rsid","chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB",
                       "frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"))

# arreglo los nombres de las columnas para que quede más claro
setnames(laa,
         old = c("frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"),
         new = c("beta_LAA","se_LAA","p_LAA"))

# uno por SNP
merged <- merge(
  laa, stroke,
  by = c("chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB"),
  all = FALSE
)

head(merged)
dim(merged)
# PARA SABER EL % DE LA MISMA DIRECCIÓN DEL EFECTO
merged[, same_direction := sign(beta_LAA) == sign(beta_STROKE)]
mean(merged$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
# CORRELACIÓN DE BETAS PARA VER CÓMO DE PARECIDAS SON
cor(merged$beta_LAA, merged$beta_STROKE, use = "complete.obs")
# VER SI LOS SNP MÁS SIGNIFICATIVOS DE CE, REPLICAN EN STROKE
top_laa <- merged[order(p_LAA)][1:1000]
mean(top_laa$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
summary(top_laa$p_STROKE)


hits_laa <- merged[p_LAA < 1e-6][order(p_LAA)]
hits_laa[, .(chromosome, position, alleleA, alleleB, beta_LAA, p_LAA, beta_STROKE, p_STROKE)][1:30]

#crear PLOT
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(1)
plot_df <- merged[sample(.N, 200000)]  # muestreo para que no reviente el plot

ggplot(plot_df, aes(beta_STROKE, beta_LAA)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  labs(x = "Beta STROKE (global)", y = "Beta LAA",
       title = "Comparación de efectos: LAA vs STROKE")

```
```{r}
### SVO ###
library(data.table)
setwd("/home/lpstroke/Documentos/Leyre Franquet/proyecto_tfg/gen1-2/Both")
getwd()
## cargo las dos dataframes, la de stroke (la general) y la de CE (una subetiología)
svo <- fread("Both/SVO_snptest_both_clean.txt",
            select = c("rsid","chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB",
                       "frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"))

# arreglo los nombres de las columnas para que quede más claro
setnames(svo,
         old = c("frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"),
         new = c("beta_SVO","se_SVO","p_SVO"))

# uno por SNP
merged <- merge(
  svo, stroke,
  by = c("chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB"),
  all = FALSE
)

head(merged)
dim(merged)
# PARA SABER EL % DE LA MISMA DIRECCIÓN DEL EFECTO
merged[, same_direction := sign(beta_SVO) == sign(beta_STROKE)]
mean(merged$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
# CORRELACIÓN DE BETAS PARA VER CÓMO DE PARECIDAS SON
cor(merged$beta_SVO, merged$beta_STROKE, use = "complete.obs")
# VER SI LOS SNP MÁS SIGNIFICATIVOS DE CE, REPLICAN EN STROKE
top_svo <- merged[order(p_SVO)][1:1000]
mean(top_svo$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
summary(top_svo$p_STROKE)


hits_svo <- merged[p_SVO < 1e-6][order(p_SVO)]
hits_svo[, .(chromosome, position, alleleA, alleleB, beta_SVO, p_SVO, beta_STROKE, p_STROKE)][1:30]

#crear PLOT
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(1)
plot_df <- merged[sample(.N, 200000)]  # muestreo para que no reviente el plot

ggplot(plot_df, aes(beta_STROKE, beta_SVO)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  labs(x = "Beta STROKE (global)", y = "Beta SVO",
       title = "Comparación de efectos: SVO vs STROKE")



```
```{r}
### FA ###
library(data.table)
setwd("/home/lpstroke/Documentos/Leyre Franquet/proyecto_tfg/gen1-2/Both")
getwd()
## cargo las dos dataframes, la de stroke (la general) y la de CE (una subetiología)
fa <- fread("Both/FA_snptest_both_clean.txt",
            select = c("rsid","chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB",
                       "frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"))

# arreglo los nombres de las columnas para que quede más claro
setnames(fa,
         old = c("frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"),
         new = c("beta_FA","se_FA","p_FA"))

# uno por SNP
merged <- merge(
  fa, stroke,
  by = c("chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB"),
  all = FALSE
)

head(merged)
dim(merged)
# PARA SABER EL % DE LA MISMA DIRECCIÓN DEL EFECTO
merged[, same_direction := sign(beta_FA) == sign(beta_STROKE)]
mean(merged$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
# CORRELACIÓN DE BETAS PARA VER CÓMO DE PARECIDAS SON
cor(merged$beta_FA, merged$beta_STROKE, use = "complete.obs")
# VER SI LOS SNP MÁS SIGNIFICATIVOS DE CE, REPLICAN EN STROKE
top_fa <- merged[order(p_FA)][1:1000]
mean(top_fa$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
summary(top_fa$p_STROKE)


hits_fa <- merged[p_FA < 1e-6][order(p_FA)]
hits_fa[, .(chromosome, position, alleleA, alleleB, beta_FA, p_FA, beta_STROKE, p_STROKE)][1:30]

#crear PLOT
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(1)
plot_df <- merged[sample(.N, 200000)]  # muestreo para que no reviente el plot

ggplot(plot_df, aes(beta_STROKE, beta_FA)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  labs(x = "Beta STROKE (global)", y = "Beta FA",
       title = "Comparación de efectos: FA vs STROKE")
```
```{r}
### OT ###
library(data.table)
setwd("/home/lpstroke/Documentos/Leyre Franquet/proyecto_tfg/gen1-2/Both")
getwd()
## cargo las dos dataframes, la de stroke (la general) y la de CE (una subetiología)
ot <- fread("Both/OT_snptest_both_clean.txt",
            select = c("rsid","chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB",
                       "frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"))

# arreglo los nombres de las columnas para que quede más claro
setnames(ot,
         old = c("frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"),
         new = c("beta_OT","se_OT","p_OT"))

# uno por SNP
merged <- merge(
  ot, stroke,
  by = c("chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB"),
  all = FALSE
)

head(merged)
dim(merged)
# PARA SABER EL % DE LA MISMA DIRECCIÓN DEL EFECTO
merged[, same_direction := sign(beta_OT) == sign(beta_STROKE)]
mean(merged$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
# CORRELACIÓN DE BETAS PARA VER CÓMO DE PARECIDAS SON
cor(merged$beta_OT, merged$beta_STROKE, use = "complete.obs")
# VER SI LOS SNP MÁS SIGNIFICATIVOS DE CE, REPLICAN EN STROKE
top_ot <- merged[order(p_OT)][1:1000]
mean(top_ot$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
summary(top_ot$p_STROKE)


hits_ot <- merged[p_OT < 1e-6][order(p_OT)]
hits_ot[, .(chromosome, position, alleleA, alleleB, beta_OT, p_OT, beta_STROKE, p_STROKE)][1:30]

#crear PLOT
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(1)
plot_df <- merged[sample(.N, 200000)]  # muestreo para que no reviente el plot

ggplot(plot_df, aes(beta_STROKE, beta_OT)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  labs(x = "Beta STROKE (global)", y = "Beta OT",
       title = "Comparación de efectos: OT vs STROKE")
```

```{r}
### UND ###
library(data.table)
setwd("/home/lpstroke/Documentos/Leyre Franquet/proyecto_tfg/gen1-2/Both")
getwd()
## cargo las dos dataframes, la de stroke (la general) y la de CE (una subetiología)
und <- fread("Both/UND_snptest_both_clean.txt",
            select = c("rsid","chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB",
                       "frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"))

# arreglo los nombres de las columnas para que quede más claro
setnames(und,
         old = c("frequentist_add_beta_1","frequentist_add_se_1","frequentist_add_pvalue"),
         new = c("beta_UND","se_UND","p_UND"))

# uno por SNP
merged <- merge(
  und, stroke,
  by = c("chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB"),
  all = FALSE
)

head(merged)
dim(merged)
# PARA SABER EL % DE LA MISMA DIRECCIÓN DEL EFECTO
merged[, same_direction := sign(beta_UND) == sign(beta_STROKE)]
mean(merged$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
# CORRELACIÓN DE BETAS PARA VER CÓMO DE PARECIDAS SON
cor(merged$beta_UND, merged$beta_STROKE, use = "complete.obs")
# VER SI LOS SNP MÁS SIGNIFICATIVOS DE CE, REPLICAN EN STROKE
top_und <- merged[order(p_UND)][1:1000]
mean(top_und$same_direction, na.rm = TRUE)
summary(top_und$p_STROKE)


hits_und <- merged[p_UND < 1e-6][order(p_UND)]
hits_und[, .(chromosome, position, alleleA, alleleB, beta_UND, p_UND, beta_STROKE, p_STROKE)][1:30]

#crear PLOT
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(1)
plot_df <- merged[sample(.N, 200000)]  # muestreo para que no reviente el plot

ggplot(plot_df, aes(beta_STROKE, beta_UND)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  labs(x = "Beta STROKE (global)", y = "Beta UND",
       title = "Comparación de efectos: UND vs STROKE")
```
```{r}
### HACER MATRIZ DE CORRELACIÓN ENTE TODAS LAS SUBETIOLOGÍAS ###
library(data.table)

subtypes <- c("CE","LAA","SVO","FA","OT","UND","STROKE")

read_beta <- function(sub){
  dt <- fread(paste0("Both/", sub, "_snptest_both_clean.txt"),
              select = c("chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB",
                         "frequentist_add_beta_1"))
  setnames(dt, "frequentist_add_beta_1", paste0("beta_", sub))
  dt
}

# leer todos
lst <- lapply(subtypes, read_beta)

# merge secuencial por SNP (chr+pos+alelos)
betas_all <- Reduce(function(x, y)
  merge(x, y, by = c("chromosome","position","alleleA","alleleB"), all = FALSE),
  lst
)

dim(betas_all)

beta_mat <- as.matrix(betas_all[, paste0("beta_", subtypes), with = FALSE])

cor_mat <- cor(beta_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
cor_mat

library(pheatmap)

pheatmap(cor_mat,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.2f",
         main = "Correlación de betas entre subetiologías")
```
```{r}
subtypes <- c("CE","LAA","SVO","FA","OT","UND")

for (sub in subtypes) {

  cat("Procesando", sub, "\n")

  dt <- fread(paste0("Both/", sub, "_snptest_both_clean.txt"),
              select = c("chromosome","position","rsid","frequentist_add_pvalue"))

  setnames(dt, "frequentist_add_pvalue", "p")

  setorder(dt, p)

  dt[, window := paste0(chromosome, ":", floor(position / 5e5))]

  top10 <- dt[!duplicated(window)][1:10]

  fwrite(top10[, .(chromosome, position, rsid, p)],
         paste0(sub, "_top10_loci_GRCh38.tsv"),
         sep = "\t")

  rm(dt, top10)
  gc()
}

```

```
```


```
